summary: Great Red Rocks show with standout Watchtower/Ship of Fools sequences; matrix recording is good overall despite a muddy audience intro.
review: The performance on this late‑summer 1987 Red Rocks run is widely praised – Jerry’s bright tone, Bob’s Masterpiece, and an electrifying post‑drums sequence (Watchtower → GDTRFB → Round n Round → Sugar Mag) keep the crowd rolling, with Ship of Fools and China Doll highlighted as monster renditions.  The matrix source captures the vibe well after the first few seconds of rough audience tape, delivering a generally clear mix that most listeners find very listenable.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise the performance; recording quality is described as good with a few minor issues, yielding an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix mix praised for its sweet balance and crowd ambience; early audience-only segment is muddy, but soundboard feed improves clarity. Some note that 1987 Red Rocks acoustics make bright guitar tone hard to capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as solid but not a barn‑burner, while the second set shines with a rock‑'n‑roll post‑drums medley and tight transitions that keep the energy high through the encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Bright, clear guitar tone (though some recordings don’t capture it fully) and a strong vocal sign‑off after Black Muddy; praised for stepping up to the mic.
band_member_comments.Bob: Creates a lovely rendition of "Masterpiece"; his rhythm work is noted as solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s piano solos are described as pouncy, errie and a highlight during the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums (Mickey) are highlighted as “out of this world” with an energetic post‑drums sequence that drives the show.
